safest_plan.entry_basis: Zero (zero_tg, 5 Jun): no precise number — entry is a confirmation trigger, not a level. SAFER: a body close ABOVE the value-area high it was rejected from, then a successful retest → long. AGGRESSIVE: a sweep of the value-area low ~4,300 that reclaims and gets acceptance back above, then breaks the high. The exact flip-high price was not stated, so no number is given.
safest_plan.stop_basis: Zero (zero_tg, 5 Jun): an ACCEPTED close below the higher-timeframe demand zone. Price has held above demand (no close below it, not even below the mid/equilibrium). The 4,300 value-area-low is the line in the sand — a sweep that RECLAIMS keeps the long valid; an accepted close that stays below ~4,300 / the demand below it kills the thesis. 4,300 is the explicit VAL; the demand floor itself sits just under it (estimated).

stance_by_source.summary: Higher-timeframe demand test held with a strong reaction and no close below demand; 4-hour chart looks like accumulation, not topping. Setup is valid but has NOT triggered — wait for either a confirmed break-and-retest of the rejected high, or a dip that sweeps ~4,300 and reclaims, before going long toward 4,800/5,000.

plain_english: Only one source covers gold here (Zero, from early June), and it leans bullish: the support area he was watching held firmly and the bigger-picture structure looks like buyers quietly building positions rather than a top forming. But the trade has not actually started — he wants to see either a confirmed push above the level price was recently rejected at (then a successful pullback to it), OR a quick dip that grabs the ~4,300 floor and then snaps back above it. If price instead settles and stays below that ~4,300 area, the idea is dead. The call is also about three weeks old and gives no exact entry price, so treat it as a setup to watch, not one to chase. Action: WAIT for one of those two confirmations before buying; do nothing until then.
